title: Citrusy Chewy Malaysian Buckwheat Crumble
description: A citrusy and chewy malaysian dish featuring buckwheat flour for a hangover-cure-friendly breakfast option.

ingredients.name: 2 tablespoons honey
ingredients.notes: Use a mixture of granulated and liquid honey for better texture

ingredients.name: 3/4 cup cold water
ingredients.notes: Use to mix buckwheat flour and honey mixture

ingredients.name: 1/2 cup almond meal
ingredients.notes: For added texture, use raw almonds ground into a fine powder

ingredients.name: 1/2 teaspoon smoked paprika
ingredients.notes: To add smokiness to the dish

ingredients.name: 3 tablespoons olive oil
ingredients.notes: Use for frying and spreading on top of crumble

instructions: In a large bowl, mix buckwheat flour with honey, lemon zest, lime zest, ground cloves, and salt. Add cold water and almond meal to form a dough.
Roll the mixture into small balls and place them on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper.
Preheat oven to 180°C (350°F). Place crumble pieces in preheated oven for about 20 minutes until golden brown, then remove from oven.
While crumbles are cooling, heat olive oil in a frying pan over medium heat. Once hot, add the crumbles and fry them on both sides until crispy and slightly toasted.
Spoon the fried crumbles onto plates and drizzle with extra honey for added sweetness. Serve with fresh fruit or any other side dish of your choice.
